content.problemStatement:
Summer is at its peak in Chefland. Chef is planning to purchase a water cooler to keep his room cool. He has two options available:


Rent a cooler at the cost of XXX coins per month.
Purchase a cooler for YYY coins.
Given that the summer season will last for MMM months in Chefland, help Chef in finding whether he should rent the cooler or not.

Chef rents the cooler only if the cost of renting the cooler is **strictly less** than the cost of purchasing it. Otherwise, he purchases the cooler.

Print YES\texttt{YES}YES if Chef should **rent the cooler**, otherwise print NO\texttt{NO}NO.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain an integer TTT — the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains three integers XXX, YYY and MMM, as described in the problem statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output YES\texttt{YES}YES if Chef should rent the cooler, otherwise output NO\texttt{NO}NO.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings YeS\texttt{YeS}YeS, yEs\texttt{yEs}yEs, yes\texttt{yes}yes and YES\texttt{YES}YES will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers X, Y and M — the monthly rent cost, the purchase cost and the number of months the summer lasts.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if Chef should rent the cooler (i.e., X·M < Y), otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: rent = 5*1 = 5 < 10 → YES.\nTest case 2: rent = 5*2 = 10 is not strictly less than 10 → NO.\nTest case 3: rent = 5*3 = 15 > 10 → NO.

hidden.label: edge case where renting is cheaper

solution.approach: For each test case compute the total renting cost = X * M using 64‑bit integer arithmetic. If this value is strictly less than Y, print YES; otherwise print NO. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time computation per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        X = int(next(it)); Y = int(next(it)); M = int(next(it))
        rent = X * M
        out.append('YES' if rent < Y else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
